title: Technical and Economic Specifications of Major (Reservoir) Dams in Iran up to the End of 1971 -- Karaj, Sefid Rud, Dez, Latian, Zayandeh Rud, Karun and 12 others
source_org: Iranian Ministry of Water and Power (compiled/cited by World Bank appraisal mission)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION with mandatory visual verification. Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1975_water_supply_sewerage_vol2_annexes.pdf (152 pages, already present in archive from a prior round, not previously mined), Volume II Annex 4 Table 4.1, PDF page 31. This is a wide landscape table that `pdftotext -layout` badly garbled (columns overlapped, many digits misread e.g. 'lIP DAMS IN IRAN UP TO TiTllE_S P 1971' for the title). Rendered to PNG at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -png -r 300 -f 31 -l 31`, then split into left half (name/type/river/location/height/capacity columns) and right half (regulated capacity/generators/cultivation area/year/cost/consultants columns) using PIL crop for legibility, and both halves read directly with the Read tool. Cross-validated against independently known real-world facts for 3 of the dams (Dez height 203m/reservoir 3,340 Mm3 matches published figures almost exactly; Karaj/Amir Kabir Dam height ~180m and reservoir ~205 Mm3 matches; Karun-1/Reza Shah Kabir Dam 4x250MW=1000MW ultimate capacity matches its status as Iran's largest hydro dam at the time) -- high confidence in the transcription's accuracy as a result. A handful of individual cells have transcription ambiguity flagged directly in the notes column of data.csv (e.g. footnote-marker placement, a couple of '-'/nil vs. numeric ambiguities); none were estimated or invented.
time_coverage: 1958-1975 (dams constructed 1958-1972, one dam under construction with 1975 target completion, several dams under study with no year assigned)
frequency: single cross-section (as of end 1971, published in a 1975 World Bank report)
units: height in metres, reservoir/regulated capacity in million cubic metres, generator capacity as unit-count x kW per unit, cultivation area in 1,000 hectares, cost in million Rials
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document; underlying data originally compiled by Iran's Ministry of Water and Power
notes: THE key find of this round for the water-infrastructure/dams mission. Covers 18 dams across three categories: (1) 12 dams constructed by 1971 -- including Amir Kabir Dam (=Karaj Dam, Karaj river, 1962), Shahbanu Farah Dam (=Sefid Rud Dam, Sepidrud river, 1962), Mohammad Reza Shah-e-Pahlavi Dam (=Dez Dam, Dez river, 1963), Farahnaz-e-Pahlavi Dam (=Latian Dam, Gajerud/Jajrud river, 1968), Shah Abbas-e-Kabir Dam (Zayandeh Rud, Esfahan, 1971); (2) 2 reservoir dams under construction as of 1971 -- Reza Shah-e-Kabir Dam (Karun river at Masjed Soleyman, later renamed Shahid Abbaspour/Karun-1 Dam post-revolution, planned 1975 completion, 4x250MW=1000MW ultimate capacity making it Iran's largest hydro project of the era) and Taleqan Dam (Shahrud river); (3) 5 dams under study with no assigned completion year -- Nader Shah-e-Afshar, Jiroft, Minab, Lar, Saveh. Every dam's Pahlavi-era honorific name is preserved as printed in the source alongside the modern/common name where confidently identifiable (added by the transcriber based on river+location+specs matching, not from the source document itself -- flagged in the common_or_modern_name column, left blank where no confident identification was made). Companion datasets: wb1975water-diversion-dams-specifications (Table 4.2, smaller diversion/irrigation dams 1937-1967) and wb1975water-reservoir-water-control-forecast-by-zone (Table 4.3, regional water-supply forecasts). Part of this project's Round 38 (mining/dams/industrial subsectors) hunt.
